hook.scenario: Calculating the chances of getting exactly 2 surge-pricing rides out of 5 Uber bookings this week.

mental_model.intuition: Probability = What you want / Everything that could happen.
mental_model.description: It's just a fraction representing the frequency of a desired outcome over all possible outcomes. It always stays between 0 (impossible) and 1 (certain).

formula.statement: P(E) = (Number of favorable outcomes) / (Total number of possible outcomes) \n P(A or B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A and B)
formula.explain: The core formula requires counting. That's why P&C is a prerequisite. You count the successes using combinations, count the total using combinations, and divide.

shortcuts.title: The 'At Least One' Trick
shortcuts.example: Probability of getting at least one Head in 4 coin tosses = 1 - P(All Tails) = 1 - (1/16) = 15/16. Way faster than calculating 1, 2, 3, and 4 heads separately.

shortcuts.title: Dice Sum Combinations
shortcuts.trick: For 2 dice, the most common sum is 7 (6 ways). Sums move symmetrically: 2 and 12 have 1 way, 3 and 11 have 2 ways... up to 7 having 6 ways.
shortcuts.example: What's the probability of rolling a sum of 5? It's symmetrically 4 ways. 4/36 = 1/9.

common_mistakes.title: Adding independent probabilities instead of multiplying
common_mistakes.explanation: If rolling a 6 AND flipping a Heads, it's (1/6) * (1/2), not addition. 'AND' means multiply, 'OR' means add.

common_mistakes.title: Forgetting without replacement changes the denominator
common_mistakes.explanation: Drawing two cards one after another without replacing: First card probability is out of 52, second is out of 51.

recall_questions.q: What is the maximum value of a probability?
If P(E) = 0.3, what is P(not E)?
What is the probability of a sure event?

oa_questions.question: A bag contains 4 red and 6 black balls. 2 balls are drawn at random. What is the probability that both are red?
oa_questions.approach: Total ways = 10C_2 = 45. Favorable ways (both red) = 4C_2 = 6. Probability = 6/45 = 2/15.

oa_questions.question: Two dice are thrown together. What is the probability that the sum of numbers on the two faces is divisible by 4?
oa_questions.approach: Divisible by 4 means sums of 4, 8, 12. Sum 4 has 3 ways, Sum 8 has 5 ways, Sum 12 has 1 way. Total = 9 ways. 9/36 = 1/4.
